description: Creates a direction-reversed copy of a vector or line, flipping tip and tail while preserving magnitude, placed at a specified starting point.
utility: Create a direction-reversed copy of a vector placed at a new point

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: The vector or line to reverse

arguments.name: point
arguments.description: The new starting point for the reversed vector

alternates.description: Reverse vector direction and place at the given point
Reverse vector direction and place at (x, y)
Reverse a line direction as a vector at the given point

examples.scenario: Reverse a vector at the origin
examples.expression:
P1 = point(G, 1, 1)
P2 = point(G, 4, 3)
V = vector(G, P1, P2)
R = reverse(G, V, point(G, 0, 0))

examples.scenario: Reverse a vector at its own tip (tail-to-tail)
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
A = point(G, 3, 2)
V = vector(G, O, A)
R = reverse(G, V, point(G, 3, 2))

examples.scenario: Reverse a vector at explicit coordinates
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 2))
R = reverse(G, V, 5, 5)

examples.scenario: Reverse a line as a vector
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 4, 3)
L = line(G, A, B)
R = reverse(G, L, point(G, 0, 0))

examples.scenario: Reverse a vector direction in vector movements
examples.expression: G=g2d(at(3,6),25,range(-1,5,1),type(geometry)); vector_1 = vector(G, point(G, 2.3, 1.2), point(G, 3.2, 1.7)); rev_1 = reverse(G, vector_1)
